summary:
Fine-needle aspiration cytology is limited in its ability to distinguish between nonproliferative and proliferative breast disease due to inadequate tissue sampling and overlap in cytomorphologic findings. Definitive differentiation requires histopathological evaluation from a core biopsy, not cytology-based assessment.

Okay, I need to summarize Agent1’s reasoning based strictly on their provided content — focusing only on clinically significant points related to whether FNAC can differentiate these two types of breast pathology.

First sentence should state clearly what the limitation is regarding diagnostic capability with FNA. The second must emphasize why other methods are needed – specifically citing architectural/cellular pattern analysis being better suited for pathologists using biopsies instead of cytology.

I'll keep it concise within exactly two sentences while ensuring all critical elements about technical limits and superior alternatives appear naturally.
